creator: Connie Loizos
title: (No AI) Apple’s John Ternus will run one of the world’s most powerful companies; the job is a minefield
dc:creator: Connie Loizos
content: Apple's top job comes with almost unrivaled power and money, but it comes with plenty of baggage, too.
contentSnippet: Apple's top job comes with almost unrivaled power and money, but it comes with plenty of baggage, too.

creator: Marina Temkin
title: (No AI) AI research lab NeoCognition lands $40M seed to build agents that learn like humans

title: (No AI) AI backlash is coming for elections
author: Lauren Feiner
content:
![Graphic photo illustration of a voting sign that reads “Vote here”.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/chorus/uploads/chorus_asset/file/25334821/STK466_ELECTION_2024_CVirginia_C.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Ask Americans how they feel about AI and [most say](https://www.ipsos.com/en-us/what-americans-agree-about-ai) they have concerns. Communities have mounted resistance to data center projects, stalling them across the US. On social media, anger at AI companies and executives is unrestrained - sometimes to the point of condoning [violence](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/911778/ai-violence-sam-altman-home).

But look at the issues that most campaigns are focused on, and AI is far less prevalent, experts say.

[More than 60 percent](https://www.ipsos.com/en-us/what-americans-agree-about-ai) of both Republicans and Democrats polled by Ipsos earlier this year agree that the government should regulate AI for economic stability and public safety, and that the technology's development should slow down. Still, "when you just ask folks, 'w …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/policy/916210/ai-midterm-elections-data-centers-jobs)
contentSnippet:
Ask Americans how they feel about AI and most say they have concerns. Communities have mounted resistance to data center projects, stalling them across the US. On social media, anger at AI companies and executives is unrestrained - sometimes to the point of condoning violence.
But look at the issues that most campaigns are focused on, and AI is far less prevalent, experts say.
More than 60 percent of both Republicans and Democrats polled by Ipsos earlier this year agree that the government should regulate AI for economic stability and public safety, and that the technology's development should slow down. Still, "when you just ask folks, 'w …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Ask Americans how they feel about AI and most say they have concerns. Communities have mounted resistance to data center projects, stalling them across the US. On social media, anger at AI companies and executives is unrestrained - sometimes to the point of condoning violence. But look at the issues that most campaigns are focused […]

title: (No AI) OpenAI’s updated image generator can now pull information from the web
author: Emma Roth
content:
![An image generated by ChatGPT Images 2](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/04/chatgpt-images-2-magazine.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

An image generated by ChatGPT Images 2.0. | Image: OpenAI

OpenAI is [rolling out the latest version](https://x.com/OpenAI/status/2046670977145372771?s=20) of its AI-powered image generator with new "thinking capabilities," allowing it to search the web to help it create multiple images from a single prompt. On Tuesday, OpenAI announced that ChatGPT Images 2.0 can now create more "sophisticated" images, with improvements to its ability to follow instructions, preserve details of your choosing, and generate text.

It's powered by OpenAI's new GPT Image 2 model, with new thinking capabilities available to ChatGPT Plus, Pro, Business, and Enterprise subscribers. When a thinking model is selected, the chatbot's image generator can pull information from the w …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/916166/openai-chatgpt-images-2)

An image generated by ChatGPT Images 2.0. | Image: OpenAI

title: (No AI) Scaling Codex to enterprises worldwide
content: OpenAI launches Codex Labs, partners with with Accenture, PwC, Infosys, and others to help enterprises deploy and scale Codex across the software development lifecycle, and hits 4M Codex WAU.

creator: Reece Rogers
title: (No AI) OpenAI Beefs Up ChatGPT’s Image Generation Model
dc:creator: Reece Rogers
content: The ChatGPT Images 2.0 model is here. Our testing shows it’s better at creating more detailed images and rendering text, but it still struggles with languages other than English.
contentSnippet: The ChatGPT Images 2.0 model is here. Our testing shows it’s better at creating more detailed images and rendering text, but it still struggles with languages other than English.

creator: Lily Hay Newman
title: (No AI) Mozilla Used Anthropic’s Mythos to Find and Fix 271 Bugs in Firefox
dc:creator: Lily Hay Newman
content: The Firefox team doesn’t think emerging AI capabilities will upend cybersecurity long term, but they warn that software developers are likely in for a rocky transition.
contentSnippet: The Firefox team doesn’t think emerging AI capabilities will upend cybersecurity long term, but they warn that software developers are likely in for a rocky transition.
